purpose: Quantify image vs text vs combined contribution to AUC

key_finding.interpretation: CLIP signal strength ≈ BERT (within 0.0005). Combining = dominated strategy.

insights: 1. CLIP signal strength is comparable to BERT in BPC dataset (0.0005 difference)
2. Combining text + image is a "dominated strategy" - v4 loses in 18 of 28 categories
3. Different categories prefer different modalities (43% CLIP, 46% BERT, 11% tie)
4. CLIP excels in small-sample visual categories (+0.005 to +0.026 AUC)
production_recommendations: 1. Do NOT blindly combine multimodal features - signal redundancy causes performance degradation
2. Use dynamic feature selection per category - visual categories prefer CLIP, text-rich prefer BERT
3. For BPC overall, BERT marginally wins (0.0005), but CLIP is viable production alternative
4. For small-sample categories with rich images (fashion, art), CLIP should be the default
theoretical_insight: Dominated strategy in feature engineering: when two features capture highly correlated signals, combining them disperses the signal across both, reducing the effective gain per split in tree-based models.
